lint.note: services.yaml defines lint as a no-op placeholder.

blockingIssues.description: OHLCV gap detection/repair remains helper-only and is not wired into the production sync/backfill flows, so interior missed windows are not automatically repaired during runtime behavior.

The added repair test still stops with a deliberately unresolved gap and therefore does not prove the required end state that a post-repair re-scan can become gap-free when upstream data is available.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax or qualify mandatory curl verification in core-api-worker for internal-only service features that have no direct HTTP surface.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The ohlcv-gap-detection-repair review again surfaced that durability-helper features are forced to document a justified curl-skip because the skill requires API verification even when no route changes exist.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify scrutiny/review guidance for rerun features so reviewers explicitly prioritize current HEAD behavior over stale handoff commit references.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The ohlcv-worker-hardening review had to reconcile a rerun feature description with a handoff commit reference from earlier work; the current process leaves that precedence implicit.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a library note that gap repair is helper-only today.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected for now because the current milestone is failing on that exact behavior; documenting the limitation as settled knowledge would risk normalizing an unresolved defect.
